questions.question_text: If $x = \sqrt{225} + \sqrt{484}$, what is the value of $x^2 - [(\sqrt{225})^2 + (32)^2]$?

A park is designed in the shape of a right-angled triangle, where the two shorter sides measure 13 m and 84 m. A circular track with a uniform width of 2 m is constructed such that its outer boundary coincides with the circumference of the triangular park. Determine the perimeter of the inner boundary of this circular track.

Ram has $\text{₹}1000$ in the denomination of $\text{₹}10$, $\text{₹}20$ and $\text{₹}5$ notes. If the number of $\text{₹}10$ notes is 16 more than that of $\text{₹}20$ notes and the number of $\text{₹}5$ notes is twice the number of $\text{₹}20$ notes, find the total number of notes that Ram has.
Pooja gets a discount of 17% on a dryer whose marked price is $\text{₹}2,000$. Since she pays by credit card, she gets an additional 10% discount. How much does she pay (in $\text{₹}$)?
At present, a father is 30 years older than his son. In 12 years, the father will be three times as old as the son. What is the present age of the son?
The difference between a discount of 35% and two successive discounts of 20% and 15% on a certain marked price is $\text{₹}70$. Find the marked price (rounded off to the nearest integer).
Find the value of m which satisfies $(\frac{19}{10})^7 \times (\frac{10}{19})^6 \times (\frac{19}{10})^{10} = (\frac{10}{19})^{m + 18}$.

Two taps A and B can fill a cistern in 4 hours and 6 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 4 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill one-fourth of the empty cistern if all the taps are opened together?

Yogesh invested a certain sum of money at 10% per annum. After 2 years, he received $\text{₹}1452$ as compound interest compounded annually. Find the corresponding simple interest (in $\text{₹}$) for 2 years at the same rate.
A sum of $\text{₹}24,000$ is invested at 2% per annum compounded annually. Find the compound interest after 2 years.
Find the measure of the unknown interior angle in an irregular hexagon with angles $140^\circ, 80^\circ, 145^\circ, 155^\circ$, and $105^\circ$.

The enrolment of students in an institution increased by 10% in April compared to the number of students in March, and then it decreased by 5%, resulting in 1045 students in June. Find the number of students in March.
If 5 workers can build a wall in 12 days, how many days will 6 workers take to build the same wall, assuming they work at the same rate?
If the third proportional to 4 and 6 is used as the base of a rectangle, and the first number (4) is its height, what is the area of the rectangle?
Three numbers are in the ratio $17:13:8$, and their LCM is 7072. Their HCF is:
In a science experiment, chemicals X, Y, and Z are mixed in the ratio 5:2:3. If the total weight of the mixture is 100 grams, how much of chemical Y is present?
The angle of elevation of the top of a tree from a point on the ground is $30^\circ$. After walking 10 m towards the tree, the angle of elevation becomes $60^\circ$. Find the height of the tree.
A man is walking at 4 km/hr. A 180 m long train crosses him in 12 seconds while coming from the opposite direction. What is the speed of the train (in km/hr)?

A keg is fitted with three taps A, B and C. If opened together, all the three taps will drain the full keg in $1\frac{3}{7}$ minutes. Taps A and B together take 3 minutes to drain the keg while taps B and C together take $1\frac{1}{2}$ minutes to drain it. How long (in minutes) taps A and C together take to drain the keg?
What is the arithmetic mean of the dataset (12, 25, 37, 48, 56, 61, 73, 85, 92, 104)?

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes and on toys of the same marked price.(A) Successive discounts of 35% and 4% on any number of toys bought.(B) Successive discounts of 30%, 40% and 10% on any number of toys bought.(C) 15% discount on the first 3 toys and 5% discount on each toy thereon.(D) 2 toys free of cost on buying 4 toys.A customer wants to buy 9 toys. Which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to her?
Two solid hemispheres are joined together to make a sphere. The total surface area of solids reduced by approximately ____%.

Two trains, A and B, start simultaneously from stations Delhi and Mumbai respectively towards each other on parallel tracks. After meeting, train A takes 16 hours to reach Mumbai, and train B takes 9 hours to reach Delhi. If train A's speed is 60 km/h, what is the length of train B (in meters), given that it crosses a pole in 18 seconds?

The expenditures of R are 65% of his salary. If his salary increased by 10% and his savings increased by 10% than previous savings, his expenditures are now 60% of his salary. His present salary is $\text{₹}$_________.
The five-digit number 7a35b is divisible by 45, where a and b are positive integers. What is the value of (a + b)?
Lekhraj sells an article at a profit of 25%. If the cost price is increased by 20% and the selling price is reduced by 10%, what percentage loss will Lekhraj incur?
The average of 5 consecutive integers is 10. What is the smallest integer?
If x is the mean proportional between (a+b) and (a-b), then find the value of x.
నీటిపారుదల సహాయంతో వ్యవసాయము మరియు మానవవాసికి ఉపయోగపడే భూగర్భ నీటిని ____ అంటారు?
Which category of beneficiaries is eligible for $\text{₹}1$ lakh under Telangana's Revanth Anna Raji Mahama-Miskam La Kosam scheme launched in September 2023?
The fibrous root system, in which the primary root is short-lived and replaced by numerous adventitious roots, is a characteristic feature of which group of plants?
Which memory is fastest and located very close to the CPU?
'Effect of Proclamation of Emergency' is explicitly provided in which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India?
Which of the following regions, ruled by the Maukharies, gradually replaced Pataliputra as a political centre of North India?

Seven people L, V, H, D, E, F and T, are sitting in a row, facing north.Only four people sit to the right of V. V sits fourth from the left end. E is the immediate neighbour of D. L sits third to the left of T.How many people sit between L and H?
Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary.1. Mistake2. Mild3. Migrate4. Milk5. Minus

In a certain code language,A % B means 'A is the father of B'.A # B means 'A is the mother of B'.A @ B means 'A is the brother of B'. andA * B means 'A is the daughter of B'.Based on the above, how is B related to X if 'B = C ? O % P £ X'?
In a certain code language, 'PINS' is coded as '3247' and 'HIPS' is coded as '4627'. What is the code for 'H' in the given code language?

In the following number pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.(NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)54, 11086, 174

Refer to the following series and answer the question. (Counting to be done from left to right only.) All numbers are single-digit numbers only.)(+9) 9 7 4 3 2 7 6 5 6 9 7 8 7 6 7 6 8 7 6 (Right)How many such even numbers there, each of which is immediately preceded by a perfect square and immediately followed by an even digit? (NOTE: 1 is also a perfect square)

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that DOES NOT belong to that group?(Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)

In the following triad, each group of letters is related to the subsequent one following a certain logic. Select, from the given options, the one which follows the same logic.TRRK - RTEJ - RIKLMAPP - AMPS - JMMP

Each letter of the word 'LANGUID' is arranged in the English alphabetical order. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical order between the letter which is third from the left and the one which is third from the right in the new letter cluster thus formed?

Based on the alphabetical order, three of the following four are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that DOES NOT belong to that group?(Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)
If 2 is added to each odd digit and 1 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 97324867, which of the following digits will be first from the left in the new number thus formed?
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?ON35, MP44, KR53, ?

HLJN is related to JNLP in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, EIGK is related to GKIM. To which of the following is FJHL related, following the same logic?

Seven boxes L, M, N, P and Q are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept between box K and box P. Only one box is kept between box I and box P, where box I is kept at the topmost position. Box N is kept immediately above box K. Only two boxes are kept between box M and box Q. Which box is kept fourth from the top?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).Statements:(I) All dogs are bats.(II) No bat is a hat.Conclusions:(I) No dog is a hat.(II) Some hats are bats.
Jay ranked $16^{th}$ from the bottom and $9^{th}$ from the top in his class. How many students are there in his class?
What should come in place of the '?' in the given series?28 12 10 13 24 ?

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.Statements:(I) All clouds are water. Some water is vapor.(II) Some clouds are vapor.(III) Some vapor is water.

Akshay starts from Point A and drives 12 km towards the north. He then takes a right turn, drives 15 kms, turns right and drives 17 km. He then takes a right turn and drives 18 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 5 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and in which direction should he drive from P in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation if '$+$' and '$-$' are interchanged and '$\times$' and '$\div$' are interchanged?$805 \div 2 + 26 - 1203 \times 3 = ?$

Seven people F, G, H, I, L, M and Q are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre of the table. Only two people sit between Q and F, when counted from the right of Q. Only one person sits between L and M when counted from the right of M. Only one person sits between F and G when counted from the left of F. Only one person sits between H and M when counted from the right of M. Only one person sits between F and I. Who sits to the immediate right of L and left of O, respectively?

If '+' means '-', '-' means '$\times$', '$\times$' means '$\div$', '$\div$' means '+', then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?$18 - 3 \div 9 + 15 \times 3 = ?$

In a certain code language,'A % B' means 'A is the mother of B','A & B' means 'A is the husband of B','A @ B' means 'A is the father of B','A % B' means 'A is the son of B' and'A * B' means 'A is the daughter of B'.If G & K ? V * P # W % J, then how is J related to K?
Select the triad which follows the same pattern as that followed by the two triads given below. Both triads follow the same pattern.RN-TP-VYPL-RN-TW
What should come in place of ? in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?WTN, VFC, GKH, LPM ?

Seven boxes F, A, C, T, O, R and S are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only two boxes are kept below A. Only three boxes are kept between T and F. Only one box is kept between O and F. Only one box is kept above C. Only two boxes are kept between S and F. O is kept at someplace above C. How many boxes are kept above O?
